req.title: Manager deletes benefit
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to delete a benefit through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers a manager deleting a benefit via UI with mandatory field validation, audit record creation, and HTTP 200/201 response. I designed five tests: (1) EP positive – authenticated manager deletes a valid benefit and verifies deletion + audit; (2) NEG – unauthenticated user is rejected with 401; (3) NEG/DT – forbidden role (employee) receives 403; (4) BVA – benefit with a single-character name (minimum boundary) is deleted successfully; (5) STC – benefit transitions through 'active → deleted' state and audit record reflects final state. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create/teardown their own benefit records.

tests.name: test_manager_deletes_valid_benefit_positive
preconditions: A benefit record exists in the system
actions: Locate the benefit row and click Delete
Assert GET /benefits/{id} returns 404 or deleted status
Assert audit log contains a delete entry for the benefit id
expected: HTTP 200 or 201 returned by delete endpoint
Benefit no longer appears in the benefits list
Audit record written with action=DELETE and entity_id matching the benefit
trace: R-WEB-035: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_deletes_valid_benefit_positive(manager_page, api_client_manager, create_benefit, assert_audit):
    benefit = create_benefit(name="Health Plan", category="medical")
    benefit_id = benefit["id"]

    manager_page.goto("/benefits")
    row = manager_page.locator(f"[data-testid='benefit-row-{benefit_id}']")
    row.locator("[data-testid='delete-btn']").click()
    manager_page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()

    manager_page.wait_for_selector(f"[data-testid='benefit-row-{benefit_id}']", state="detached")

    resp = api_client_manager.get(f"/benefits/{benefit_id}")
    assert resp.status_code in (404, 200)
    if resp.status_code == 200:
        assert resp.json().get("status") == "deleted"

    assert_audit(entity_id=benefit_id, action="DELETE")

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_benefit
preconditions: No active session / no auth token
actions: POST /benefits/{id} delete endpoint without auth header
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_benefit(anon_api_client, create_benefit, assert_no_audit):
    benefit = create_benefit(name="Dental Plan", category="dental")
    benefit_id = benefit["id"]

    resp = anon_api_client.delete(f"/benefits/{benefit_id}")
    assert resp.status_code == 401

    admin_resp = <admin_api_client>.get(f"/benefits/{benefit_id}")
    assert admin_resp.status_code == 200
    assert admin_resp.json()["status"] != "deleted"

    assert_no_audit(entity_id=benefit_id, action="DELETE")

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_benefit
preconditions: User is authenticated with role=employee (not manager)
trace: R-WEB-035: error pathway 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_benefit(employee_api_client, create_benefit, assert_no_audit):
    benefit = create_benefit(name="Vision Plan", category="vision")
    benefit_id = benefit["id"]

    resp = employee_api_client.delete(f"/benefits/{benefit_id}")
    assert resp.status_code == 403

    admin_resp = <admin_api_client>.get(f"/benefits/{benefit_id}")
    assert admin_resp.status_code == 200
    assert admin_resp.json()["status"] != "deleted"

    assert_no_audit(entity_id=benefit_id, action="DELETE")

tests.name: test_manager_deletes_benefit_with_minimum_name_length
preconditions: A benefit with a single-character name exists (minimum valid boundary)
actions: Create benefit with name='A' (1 character, minimum boundary)
Locate the benefit row and click Delete
expected: Benefit with single-char name is deleted
Audit record written with entity_id and action=DELETE
trace: R-WEB-035: positive delete path at name length boundary=1
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_deletes_benefit_with_minimum_name_length(manager_page, api_client_manager, create_benefit, assert_audit):
    benefit = create_benefit(name="A", category="medical")
    benefit_id = benefit["id"]

    manager_page.goto("/benefits")
    row = manager_page.locator(f"[data-testid='benefit-row-{benefit_id}']")
    assert row.is_visible(), "Benefit with 1-char name must appear in list"

    row.locator("[data-testid='delete-btn']").click()
    manager_page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()

    manager_page.wait_for_selector(f"[data-testid='benefit-row-{benefit_id}']", state="detached")

    resp = api_client_manager.get(f"/benefits/{benefit_id}")
    assert resp.status_code in (404, 200)
    if resp.status_code == 200:
        assert resp.json().get("status") == "deleted"

    assert_audit(entity_id=benefit_id, action="DELETE")

tests.name: test_benefit_state_transitions_active_to_deleted
actions: Verify benefit is in state=active via GET /benefits/{id}
Navigate to /benefits and trigger delete action
Verify benefit state transitions to state=deleted or HTTP 404
Verify audit record action=DELETE and previous_state=active
expected: Benefit transitions from active to deleted
Audit record reflects state transition with previous_state=active
HTTP 200 or 201 on delete; benefit absent or marked deleted on subsequent GET
trace: R-WEB-035: benefit is deleted (state transition active->deleted)
R-WEB-035: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_benefit_state_transitions_active_to_deleted(manager_page, api_client_manager, create_benefit, assert_audit):
    benefit = create_benefit(name="Life Insurance", category="life", status="active")
    benefit_id = benefit["id"]

    pre = api_client_manager.get(f"/benefits/{benefit_id}")
    assert pre.status_code == 200
    assert pre.json()["status"] == "active"

    manager_page.goto("/benefits")
    row = manager_page.locator(f"[data-testid='benefit-row-{benefit_id}']")
    row.locator("[data-testid='delete-btn']").click()
    manager_page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()
    manager_page.wait_for_selector(f"[data-testid='benefit-row-{benefit_id}']", state="detached")

    post = api_client_manager.get(f"/benefits/{benefit_id}")
    assert post.status_code in (404, 200)
    if post.status_code == 200:
        assert post.json()["status"] == "deleted"

    assert_audit(entity_id=benefit_id, action="DELETE", previous_state="active")
